standard.notes: 'Aller à l'école' = to go to school. 'L'école primaire' = primary school, 'l'école maternelle' = nursery school. In France, school is mandatory from age 3.
examples: Les enfants vont à l'école le matin.
The children go to school in the morning.
L'école est fermée le mercredi après-midi.
The school is closed on Wednesday afternoon.

standard.notes: A standard school supply in France. 'Un cahier de brouillon' = a rough/draft notebook. 'Un cahier de textes' = a homework diary.
examples: Ouvrez votre cahier à la page dix.
Open your notebook to page ten.
J'ai écrit la leçon dans mon cahier.
I wrote the lesson in my notebook.

standard.notes: Short for 'le stylo à bille' (ballpoint pen). 'Un stylo-plume' = a fountain pen. 'Un crayon' = a pencil (different from a pen).
examples: Tu as un stylo, s'il te plaît ?
Do you have a pen, please?
J'écris avec un stylo bleu.
I write with a blue pen.

standard.notes: Conjugated like 'prendre'. Can mean both 'to learn' (apprendre le français) and 'to teach' (apprendre quelque chose à quelqu'un). Past participle: appris.
examples: J'apprends le français à l'école.
I learn French at school.
On a appris beaucoup de choses aujourd'hui.
We learned a lot of things today.

standard.notes: Often shortened to 'le/la prof' in informal speech. Traditionally masculine, but 'la professeure' is increasingly used for women. Used for secondary school and university teachers.
examples: Le professeur explique la leçon.
The teacher explains the lesson.
Notre prof de maths est très sympa.
Our maths teacher is very nice.

advanced.notes: 'Aller à l'école' = to go to school. 'L'école primaire' = primary school, 'l'école maternelle' = nursery school. In France, school is mandatory from age 3.
examples: Les enfants vont à l'école le matin.
The children go to school in the morning.
L'école est fermée le mercredi après-midi.
The school is closed on Wednesday afternoon.

advanced.notes: A standard school supply in France. 'Un cahier de brouillon' = a rough/draft notebook. 'Un cahier de textes' = a homework diary.
examples: Ouvrez votre cahier à la page dix.
Open your notebook to page ten.
J'ai écrit la leçon dans mon cahier.
I wrote the lesson in my notebook.

advanced.notes: Short for 'le stylo à bille' (ballpoint pen). 'Un stylo-plume' = a fountain pen. 'Un crayon' = a pencil (different from a pen).
examples: Tu as un stylo, s'il te plaît ?
Do you have a pen, please?
J'écris avec un stylo bleu.
I write with a blue pen.

advanced.notes: Conjugated like 'prendre'. Can mean both 'to learn' (apprendre le français) and 'to teach' (apprendre quelque chose à quelqu'un). Past participle: appris.
examples: J'apprends le français à l'école.
I learn French at school.
On a appris beaucoup de choses aujourd'hui.
We learned a lot of things today.

advanced.notes: Often shortened to 'le/la prof' in informal speech. Traditionally masculine, but 'la professeure' is increasingly used for women. Used for secondary school and university teachers.
examples: Le professeur explique la leçon.
The teacher explains the lesson.
Notre prof de maths est très sympa.
Our maths teacher is very nice.

advanced.notes: False friend alert! 'La bibliothèque' = the library, NOT the bookshop. 'La librairie' = the bookshop. 'Aller à la bibliothèque' = to go to the library.
examples: Je vais à la bibliothèque pour étudier.
I go to the library to study.
La bibliothèque a beaucoup de livres en français.
The library has many books in French.

advanced.notes: 'Passer un examen' = to TAKE an exam (not to pass it!). 'Réussir un examen' = to pass an exam. 'Rater un examen' = to fail an exam.
examples: J'ai un examen de maths demain.
I have a maths exam tomorrow.
Il a réussi son examen !
He passed his exam!

dialogue: Salut ! Tu as fait quoi à l'école aujourd'hui ?
J'ai étudié les maths ce matin. Et toi ?
Moi, j'ai appris des nouveaux mots en français.
Super ! Tu as écrit dans ton cahier ?
Oui, j'ai écrit tous les mots avec un stylo rouge.
Oui, il a dit que j'ai bien travaillé !
Génial ! On a bien travaillé tous les deux aujourd'hui.

questions.question: What did speaker B study this morning?
What did speaker A learn at school?
What did the teacher say about speaker A's work?

standard.summary: Two students compare their school days. Speaker B studied maths in the morning, while speaker A learned new French vocabulary words and wrote them all in their notebook with a red pen. The teacher praised speaker A for working well, and both agree they had a productive day.

dialogue: Quelle journée ! J'ai eu un examen de maths ce matin.
Assez difficile, oui. J'ai révisé hier soir à la bibliothèque, mais il y avait des questions surprises.
Moi, j'ai passé l'après-midi à lire un roman pour le cours de littérature.
Tu as lu tout le livre ?
Non, j'ai lu seulement cent pages. J'ai pris des notes dans mon cahier.
Bonne idée ! Le professeur a demandé un résumé aussi ?
Oui, on a écrit un résumé en classe. J'ai appris beaucoup de vocabulaire nouveau.
Au moins, on a bien travaillé aujourd'hui !
C'est vrai. Demain, j'ai un examen d'histoire — il faut que je révise ce soir.

questions.question: Where did speaker A study the night before?
How many pages did speaker B read?
What exam does speaker B have tomorrow?

advanced.summary: Two students discuss their busy school day. Speaker A had a difficult maths exam despite studying at the library the night before. Speaker B spent the afternoon reading a novel for literature class — a hundred pages — and took notes. The teacher asked for a written summary in class. Speaker B has a history exam the next day and needs to revise that evening.

standard.title: Passé Composé with Avoir (Introduction)
standard.explanation:
The passé composé is the most common past tense in French. It describes completed actions — things that happened and are finished.

**How to form it:**
subject + **avoir** (conjugated) + **past participle**

**Avoir conjugation:**
- j'ai, tu as, il/elle a, nous avons, vous avez, ils/elles ont

**Regular past participles:**
- **-ER verbs → -é:** manger → mang**é**, regarder → regard**é**, étudier → étudi**é**
- **-IR verbs → -i:** finir → fin**i**, choisir → chois**i**, réussir → réuss**i**
- **-RE verbs → -u:** attendre → attend**u**, vendre → vend**u**, perdre → perd**u**

**Examples:**
- J'ai mangé une pomme. (I ate an apple.)
- Tu as fini tes devoirs. (You finished your homework.)
- Elle a attendu le bus. (She waited for the bus.)

**Key point:** The past participle does NOT change — it stays the same regardless of the subject (with avoir). J'ai mangé, elle a mangé, ils ont mangé — always 'mangé'.

examples.french: Tu as écrit dans ton cahier ?
examples.english: Did you write in your notebook?

drills.question: Complete: J'ai ___ un livre hier. (I read a book yesterday. — past participle of lire)
drills.explanation: 'Lire' has an irregular past participle: lu. J'ai lu = I read (past).

drills.question: What is the past participle of 'manger'?
drills.explanation: -ER verbs form their past participle with -é: manger → mangé.

drills.question: Complete: Elle ___ fini ses devoirs. (She finished her homework.)
drills.explanation: Passé composé = avoir + past participle. 'Elle' takes 'a': elle a fini.

drills.question: How do you say 'We studied' in French?
drills.explanation: Étudier is an -ER verb: past participle = étudié. With avoir: nous avons étudié.

drills.question: Complete: Tu as ___ le bus ? (Did you wait for the bus? — past participle of attendre)
drills.explanation: -RE verbs form their past participle with -u: attendre → attendu.

advanced.title: Passé Composé with Avoir — Formation, Common Past Participles & Negation
advanced.explanation:
**Formation:** subject + avoir (conjugated) + past participle

**Regular past participles:**
- -ER → -é: étudié, mangé, regardé
- -IR → -i: fini, choisi, réussi
- -RE → -u: attendu, vendu, perdu

**Common irregular past participles (must memorise):**
| Infinitive | Past Participle | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| avoir | **eu** | had |
| être | **été** | been |
| faire | **fait** | done/made |
| prendre | **pris** | taken |
| apprendre | **appris** | learned |
| mettre | **mis** | put |
| voir | **vu** | seen |
| lire | **lu** | read |
| écrire | **écrit** | written |
| dire | **dit** | said |

**Negation in passé composé:**
'Ne' goes before avoir, 'pas' goes after avoir (before the past participle):
- Je **n'ai pas** mangé. (I didn't eat.)
- Il **n'a pas** fini. (He didn't finish.)
- Nous **n'avons pas** compris. (We didn't understand.)

**Useful time expressions with passé composé:**
- **hier** (yesterday), **avant-hier** (the day before yesterday)
- **la semaine dernière** (last week), **le mois dernier** (last month)
- **ce matin** (this morning — if it's now afternoon)
- **il y a + time** (ago): il y a deux jours (two days ago)

**Questions:**
- Est-ce que tu as fini ? / Tu as fini ? (Did you finish?)
- Qu'est-ce que tu as fait ? (What did you do?)

examples.french: J'ai appris le français à l'école.
Elle n'a pas lu le livre.
Qu'est-ce que vous avez fait hier ?

drills.question: Complete: J'ai ___ mes devoirs hier soir. (I did my homework last night. — past participle of faire)
drills.explanation: 'Faire' has an irregular past participle: fait.

drills.question: What is the past participle of 'prendre'?
drills.explanation: 'Prendre' → 'pris'. All compounds follow: apprendre → appris, comprendre → compris.

drills.question: Complete: Elle n'a pas ___ la question. (She didn't understand the question. — past participle of comprendre)
drills.explanation: 'Comprendre' → 'compris' (like prendre → pris). Negation: n'a pas compris.

drills.question: How do you say 'I didn't eat' in French?
drills.explanation: Negation wraps 'avoir': ne + ai + pas, then the past participle follows: n'ai pas mangé.

drills.question: Complete: Nous avons ___ un film intéressant. (We saw an interesting film. — past participle of voir)
drills.explanation: 'Voir' has an irregular past participle: vu.

drills.question: Which sentence means 'What did you do yesterday?'?
options: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait hier ?
Qu'est-ce que tu vas faire hier ?
Qu'est-ce que tu avais fait hier ?
drills.correctAnswer: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait hier ?
drills.explanation: Use passé composé for completed past actions: 'tu as fait' (you did).

drills.question: Complete: Il a ___ une lettre à son ami. (He wrote a letter to his friend. — past participle of écrire)
drills.explanation: 'Écrire' has an irregular past participle: écrit.

drills.question: What does 'il y a deux jours' mean?
drills.explanation: 'Il y a' + time expression = ago. 'Il y a deux jours' = two days ago.

standard.question: What does 'le cahier' mean?
standard.explanation: 'Le cahier' is a notebook or exercise book. 'Le stylo' is a pen.

standard.question: Fill in the blank: J'ai ___ le français à l'école. (I learned French at school. — past participle of apprendre)
standard.explanation: 'Apprendre' has an irregular past participle: appris (like prendre → pris).

standard.question: How do you say 'the teacher' in French?
standard.explanation: 'Le professeur' (often shortened to 'le/la prof') is the teacher.

standard.question: Fill in the blank: Elle ___ étudié ce matin. (She studied this morning.)
standard.explanation: Passé composé: avoir (conjugated) + past participle. 'Elle' → 'a'. Elle a étudié.

standard.question: What is the past participle of 'finir'?
standard.explanation: -IR verbs form their past participle with -i: finir → fini.

advanced.question: What does 'la bibliothèque' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'La bibliothèque' is a library. A bookshop is 'la librairie' — a common false friend!

advanced.question: Fill in the blank: Il n'a pas ___ le livre. (He didn't read the book. — past participle of lire)
advanced.explanation: 'Lire' has an irregular past participle: lu. Negation: n'a pas lu.

advanced.question: What does 'l'examen' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'L'examen' means the exam or test. 'Passer un examen' = to take an exam (not to pass it!).

advanced.question: Fill in the blank: Qu'est-ce que tu as ___ hier ? (What did you do yesterday? — past participle of faire)
advanced.explanation: 'Faire' → 'fait'. This is one of the most common irregular past participles.

advanced.question: How do you say 'two days ago' in French?
advanced.explanation: 'Il y a' + duration = ago. 'Dans deux jours' = in two days (future).

advanced.question: Fill in the blank: Nous avons ___ beaucoup de vocabulaire. (We learned a lot of vocabulary. — past participle of apprendre)
advanced.explanation: 'Apprendre' → 'appris'. Compounds of prendre all follow this pattern.

standard.scenario: You meet a classmate after school. Tell them about two or three things you did at school today using the passé composé.
keyPhrases: J'ai appris / étudié / écrit… (I learned / studied / wrote…)
Et toi, tu as fait quoi ? (And you, what did you do?)
standard.modelAnswer: Salut ! Aujourd'hui, j'ai étudié les maths et j'ai appris des mots nouveaux en français. J'ai écrit dans mon cahier avec mon nouveau stylo. Le professeur a dit que j'ai bien travaillé ! Et toi, tu as fait quoi ?
standard.modelAnswerExplanation: The model answer uses multiple passé composé forms naturally (ai étudié, ai appris, ai écrit, a dit, ai travaillé), mentions school vocabulary (cahier, stylo, professeur), and ends with a question to keep the conversation going.
standard.tip: Use 'j'ai' + past participle for each thing you did. Remember: -ER verbs → -é, -IR verbs → -i.

advanced.scenario: Describe a full school day to a French friend. Include what subjects you studied, what you read or wrote, and how an exam went. Use a variety of past participles and time expressions.
keyPhrases: Ce matin, j'ai… / Cet après-midi, j'ai… (This morning, I… / This afternoon, I…)
J'ai passé un examen de… (I took a… exam.)
J'ai lu / écrit / appris / fait… (I read / wrote / learned / did…)
La semaine dernière / Hier / Il y a deux jours (Last week / Yesterday / Two days ago)
advanced.modelAnswer: Ce matin, j'ai passé un examen de maths — c'était assez difficile, mais j'ai répondu à toutes les questions. Ensuite, j'ai lu cinquante pages d'un roman à la bibliothèque et j'ai pris des notes dans mon cahier. Cet après-midi, le professeur a expliqué une leçon de grammaire et j'ai appris des verbes irréguliers. Hier, j'ai aussi écrit un résumé pour le cours de littérature.
advanced.modelAnswerExplanation: The model answer uses varied time expressions (ce matin, ensuite, cet après-midi, hier), multiple irregular past participles (passé, lu, pris, appris, écrit), and school vocabulary (examen, bibliothèque, cahier, professeur). It creates a natural narrative arc across the school day.
